description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a long, straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, ideal for thrusting and slashing. The blade is metallic, likely steel, with a subtle reflective sheen and a darkened central fuller running along its length. The crossguard is simple and straight, made of a darker metal or possibly brass, providing hand protection. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a dark material, possibly leather, and ends in a rounded pommel for balance and grip security. The overall design is utilitarian with no visible ornamentation, emphasizing function over decoration. The sword is well-proportioned and appears suitable for one-handed use, typical of arming swords seen in historical European contexts.
description: A straight, double-edged medieval sword with a metallic blade, simple crossguard, and a dark, cylindrical handle ending in a rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and handle with pommel.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the blade, crossguard, and handle.
description_view_3: View from the pommel end, showing the circular cross-section of the handle and the alignment of the blade.
